# claude-loop

*Generated by Claude (claude-opus-4-6)*

A bash script that runs Claude Code headlessly against a directory of plan files, iterating through tasks one at a time. Sometimes called a "Ralph Wiggum loop" — you hand over the keys and walk away.

## Installation

This script is managed via [GNU Stow](https://www.gnu.org/software/stow/) as part of the `zsh` dotfiles package. Running `stow -t $HOME zsh` from `dotfiles/` symlinks it to `~/.local/bin/claude-loop`. Ensure `~/.local/bin` is on your PATH (this is handled by `.zshrc`).

## Plans directory structure

Create a plans directory in your target repository:

```
plans/
  context.md           # Project context — always included in the prompt
  requirements.md      # Acceptance criteria — always included in the prompt
  plan-01-setup.md     # First task
  plan-02-feature.md   # Second task
  plan-03-tests.md     # Third task
```

- `context.md` and `requirements.md` are optional. When present, their contents are prepended to every task prompt.
- Task files must match the pattern `plan-*.md` and are executed in lexicographic order.
- Each task file should describe a single, focused piece of work.

### Status tracking

The script creates a `.status/` directory inside the plans directory to track progress:

```
plans/.status/
  01-setup.status      # "complete", "running", or "failed:<exit-code>"
  01-setup.log         # Full Claude output for that task
  02-feature.status
  02-feature.log
```

Add `.status/` to your `.gitignore`.

## Usage

```bash
# Run from within the target repository
cd ~/my-project

# Execute all pending tasks
claude-loop ./plans

# Preview what would run without executing
claude-loop ./plans --dry-run

# Check task statuses
claude-loop ./plans --status

# Re-run everything from scratch
claude-loop ./plans --reset

# Re-run a specific task (matches against the task ID)
claude-loop ./plans --reset-task 01

# Use a specific model
claude-loop ./plans --model sonnet

# Override the permission mode (default: --dangerously-skip-permissions)
claude-loop ./plans --permission-mode auto

# Cap API spend at $5 per task
claude-loop ./plans --max-budget 5
```

## Behaviour

- Tasks run sequentially in sorted order.
- Completed tasks are skipped on subsequent runs.
- The loop stops on the first failed task. Fix the issue and re-run to continue from where it left off.
- Claude's output is written to both stdout and a log file per task in `.status/`.
- By default, Claude runs with `--dangerously-skip-permissions` — it can edit files, run commands, and make changes without prompting. Use `--permission-mode` to restrict this.

## Options

| Flag | Description |
|---|---|
| `--dry-run` | Show what would be executed without running Claude |
| `--status` | Show current task statuses and exit |
| `--reset` | Clear all task statuses |
| `--reset-task PATTERN` | Clear status for tasks matching the pattern |
| `--model MODEL` | Override the Claude model |
| `--permission-mode MODE` | Override permission mode (default: `dangerously-skip-permissions`) |
| `--max-budget USD` | Cap API spend per task in dollars |
| `--help` | Show help message |

## Writing good plan files

- Keep each task focused on one thing. Smaller tasks produce better results than large ambiguous ones.
- Include enough detail that Claude can work without clarification — file paths, function names, expected behaviour.
- Use `context.md` for information that applies to all tasks (tech stack, conventions, architecture).
- Use `requirements.md` for acceptance criteria that all tasks should satisfy.
- Order tasks so that later tasks can build on earlier ones, but avoid tight dependencies on *how* earlier tasks were implemented.
